mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not positive in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, the zero lower bound constraint forces investors to bid up equities as the risk-free alternative yields negligible returns, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or insignificantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) in at least 60% of months when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Accommodative monetary policy (low FEDFUNDS) combined with weak industrial production (low INDPRO) jointly signal a central bank put that suppresses downside equity risk and drives positive S&P 500 returns through risk premium compression.
failure_condition: In joint episodes of FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and INDPRO below its 12-month moving average, 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or occur less than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment environment, month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) indicate resilient consumer demand that supports corporate top-line growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 7.0%, positive month-over-month RSAFS changes are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) signal contracting manufacturing output and earnings compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO declines are not negative in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 900 thousand units signal impaired construction activity and weak household formation, which drags on cyclical demand and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: In periods when HOUST < 900, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) when above 7% signals improving labor market tightness that boosts wage and consumption expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 7.0%, month-over-month declines in UNRATE are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes real corporate margins and prompts expectations of future monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL are not negative in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.
